events.note: Knockout stage begins, 5 p.m. ET.

events.title: City budget adoption deadline
events.note: The Council and the mayor adopt the budget. The Charter's hard date is June 5 (section 254(d)); in practice adoption lands by June 30, the last day of the fiscal year, and the fiscal 2027 budget was adopted June 30, 2026.

events.title: Rikers land transfer deadline
events.note: Under the same law that sets the closure date, the mayor must transfer every portion of Rikers not in active use to the Department of Citywide Administrative Services every six months. The clock runs Jan. 1 and July 1.

events.title: Liberty Weekend, 40 years on
events.note: In July 1986 the Statue of Liberty's centennial and rededication drew the largest tall-ship parade in modern history, with Reagan presiding.

events.title: America's 250th birthday
events.note: New York Harbor hosts the largest fleet of tall ships and naval vessels ever assembled, echoing the 1976 bicentennial and 1986 Liberty Weekend. Coincides with the 50th Macy's Fourth of July fireworks.

events.title: World Cup round of 16 at MetLife Stadium
events.note: Knockout stage, 4 p.m. ET.

events.title: The Declaration is read to Washington's troops — 250th anniversary
events.note: The Declaration of Independence was read aloud to Continental Army troops assembled on the Common on July 9, 1776, greeted by three cheers.

events.title: The statue of King George III is toppled — 250th anniversary
events.note: After the Declaration was read, a crowd rushed to Bowling Green and pulled down the gilded lead equestrian statue of King George III.

events.title: Triborough Bridge opens — 90th anniversary
events.note: Robert Moses's Triborough Bridge opened, linking Manhattan, Queens and the Bronx in a vast web of spans and viaducts.

events.title: The 1976 Democratic convention, 50 years on
events.note: Fifty years since the convention at Madison Square Garden nominated Carter, in the depths of the city's fiscal crisis.

events.title: First Rikers remediation action plan due
events.note: Nicholas Deml, appointed the court's Nunez remediation manager on Feb. 23, 2026, is scheduled to submit his first remediation action plan in mid-July. The court declined to impose a receivership in May 2025 and installed a remediation manager instead. Date approximate within mid-July.

events.title: TWA Flight 800 explodes after takeoff from JFK — 30th anniversary
events.note: TWA Flight 800 exploded off Long Island shortly after leaving John F. Kennedy International Airport, killing all 230 people aboard. The investigation traced the cause to a center fuel-tank explosion and drove lasting changes to aviation fuel-tank safety rules.

events.title: World Cup final at MetLife Stadium
events.note: 3 p.m. ET. The largest single sporting event ever hosted in the New York region. Weeks of security, transit and crowd planning converge on this day.

events.title: July 21, 2026, Public Hearing Agenda

events.note: Environmental Protection · Address Not Listed In The Dropdown

events.title: New York adopts first US zoning resolution — 110th anniversary
events.note: New York enacted the nation's first comprehensive citywide zoning law, regulating building height, bulk and use.

events.note: Board of Standards and Appeals · 22 Reade Street

events.title: Notice of Availability of Final Scope of Work, Completion of a Draft Environmental Impact Statement and Public Hearing
events.note: Sanitation

events.title: Son of Sam, 50 years on
events.note: On July 29, 1976, David Berkowitz began the series of attacks that terrorized the city for a year.

events.title: Black Tom explosion rocks the harbor — 110th anniversary
events.note: German saboteurs blew up a massive munitions depot on Black Tom Island in New York Harbor, shattering windows across the city.

events.title: Transportation and the Shape of Cities, by Christof Spieler and others
events.note: Argues each travel mode — walking, micromobility, cars, rail, transit — has a geometry that dictates the shape of the city built around it. (Island Press)

events.title: Committee on Land Use
events.note: 250 Broadway - 8th Floor - Hearing Room 1

events.note: 250 Broadway - 8th Floor - Hearing Room 1

events.note: Housing Preservation and Development

events.title: DCWP NOH Requirements for HVFHV Services and Protections for Drivers
events.note: Consumer and Worker Protection

events.title: DCWP NOH Junk Fees
events.note: Consumer and Worker Protection

events.title: The Great Driving Right Show, by the Zetkin Collective
events.note: How the far right built a mass politics around defending the car, from anti-congestion-charge campaigns to farmers' protests. (Verso)

events.title: Subcommittee on Zoning and Franchises
events.note: 250 Broadway - 8th Floor - Hearing Room 1

events.title: FOR ACQUISITION - portions of  Block 3264, Lot 20; Block 3238, Lots 50, 52, and 126; and Block 3245, Lot 12
events.note: Citywide Administrative Services

events.title: Stated meeting of the City Council
events.note: Council Chambers - City Hall
why: stated meeting of the full Council

events.title: Driver Relief Penalty Reduction and Medallion Relief Program Rule Proposal.
events.note: Taxi and Limousine Commission · 33 Beaver Street
why: Taxi and Limousine Commission sets policy the public feels directly

events.title: Anarchitecture After Everything, by Jack Halberstam
events.note: Connects the 1970s practice of cutting apart buildings to trans aesthetics and anti-urban-renewal politics. (MIT Press)

events.title: The Other Housing Crisis, edited by Todd Swanstrom and others
events.note: Argues affordability is not only about building new units but about stopping the decay of the older housing people already live in. (Island Press)

events.title: The Battle of Long Island — 250th anniversary
events.note: The Battle of Long Island on August 27, 1776, was the largest battle of the Revolution and a British victory that gave them New York for the rest of the war.

events.title: Local Law 97 extended filing deadline
events.note: The final deadline for buildings that paid the $60 extension fee in June.

events.title: U.S. Open begins
events.note: The main draw begins on a Sunday under the 15-day format and runs through Sept. 13. Qualifying and Fan Week start the week before. Major tourism and transit impact for Queens.

events.title: One year until Rikers must close
events.note: Administrative Code section 4-215, from Local Law 16 of 2021, requires that by Aug. 31, 2027 all of Rikers Island be transferred out of the Correction Department's control and no longer used to house people. This marks exactly one year out.

events.title: A Fragile Alliance, by James J. Barney
events.note: Reassesses David Dinkins, New York's first Black mayor, casting 1989-93 as the test case for the multiracial coalition politics still being fought over. (Fordham)

events.title: Fire in the Heart of the City, by David Conrad-Pérez
events.note: How the 1911 Triangle fire set off a fight between New York's elite and labor organizers over who controls the city's disaster response. (NYU Press)

events.title: Deadline for charter proposals to reach the November ballot
events.note: Mamdani's Commission on Government Efficiency finished its 10 public hearings and published a preliminary staff report on July 2. To put charter amendments before voters on Nov. 3, it must file them with the city clerk at least 60 days beforehand. No filing date has been announced; this is the outer bound the law implies, not a scheduled event. The preliminary report points at outdoor dining, permitting, procurement, bus and street infrastructure and a rainy day fund.

events.title: Luigi Mangione's state murder trial begins
events.note: The state trial for the killing of UnitedHealthcare chief executive Brian Thompson in Midtown. Originally set for June 8 and moved to September by Judge Gregory Carro. The separate federal case, which carries the death penalty, follows in January.

events.title: Free 2-K launches: 2,000 seats
events.note: The first free 2-K seats open in four communities, announced with Gov. Kathy Hochul in March, expanding to universality within four years. Reporting puts services beginning in September; no exact launch date has been announced, so this is pegged to the start of the school year.

events.title: First day of school
events.note: The 2026-27 school year begins the day after Labor Day. Some 800,000 children return to K-12 public school classrooms, a number that has fallen roughly 10% since 2020.

events.title: A Right to Housing?, by Samuel Stein
events.note: The author of Capital City on what a socialist city administration would actually have to do — and what real estate power would block — to make housing a right. (Verso)

events.title: Sez I to Myself, by Frank and Malachy McCourt
events.note: Uncollected essays, many first run in Upper West Side neighborhood papers, on immigrant New York, pubs, classrooms and 9/11. (Abrams)

events.title: FDNY ceremony for the 25th anniversary of 9/11
events.note: The official Fire Department ceremony honoring the 343 members killed on Sept. 11, 2001 and the more than 400 who have since died of World Trade Center-related illness. Held at the Staten Island FerryHawks' stadium as part of a year-long commemoration.

events.title: Stated meeting of the City Council
events.note: Council Chambers - City Hall
why: stated meeting of the full Council

events.title: MWBE_7-858-0662A - HYBRID CLOUD SECURITY ARCHITECT
events.note: Information Technology and Telecommunications

events.title: 9/11 attacks destroy the World Trade Center — 25th anniversary
events.note: Al-Qaeda hijackers crashed two jetliners into the twin towers of the World Trade Center, collapsing both and killing 2,753 people in New York City. It was the deadliest terrorist attack in U.S. history.

events.title: "The Black Crook" invents the American musical — 160th anniversary
events.note: A five-and-a-half-hour spectacle fusing melodrama, ballet and song opened at Niblo's Garden and is widely credited as the first American musical, running a record 474 performances.

events.title: The British land at Kip's Bay — 250th anniversary
events.note: British warships and troops landed at Kip's Bay on Manhattan's East River shore on September 15, 1776, scattering American militia.

events.title: Committee on Education
events.note: Jointly with the Committee on Oversight and Investigations · Council Chambers - City Hall
why: subject matter of broad public interest

events.title: Committee on Oversight and Investigations
events.note: Jointly with the Committee on Education. · Council Chambers - City Hall
why: subject matter of broad public interest

events.title: Max Bond, by Brian D. Goldstein
events.note: The first biography of J. Max Bond Jr., the Black architect behind the King Center and the 9/11 Memorial Museum. (Princeton)

events.title: The Battle of Harlem Heights — 250th anniversary
events.note: On September 16, 1776, Washington won his first battlefield victory of the war at Harlem Heights in upper Manhattan.

events.title: The Met moves to Lincoln Center — 60th anniversary
events.note: The Metropolitan Opera opened its new house at Lincoln Center, leaving the 1883 building on 39th Street for Wallace Harrison's grand modern hall.

events.title: Chelsea bombing injures 31 — 10th anniversary
events.note: A pressure-cooker bomb exploded on West 23rd Street in Chelsea, injuring 31 people. Ahmad Khan Rahimi was arrested days later and sentenced to life in prison.

events.title: Mayor's Management Report due
events.note: The charter-mandated scorecard for every agency: trash, crime, service delivery. Required by Charter section 12 no later than Sept. 17. This is the first full fiscal year Mamdani's agencies are judged on, though the year began before he took office.

events.title: The New-York Daily Times debuts — 175th anniversary
events.note: Henry Raymond and George Jones published the first issue of the New-York Daily Times, later The New York Times.

events.title: The Great Fire of 1776 — 250th anniversary
events.note: A fire burned through the night into September 21, 1776, destroying a large swath of British-occupied New York including Trinity Church.

events.title: Nathan Hale is hanged as a spy — 250th anniversary
events.note: The British hanged 21-year-old Continental officer Nathan Hale as a spy in occupied New York on September 22, 1776.

events.title: United Nations General Assembly: leaders' week
events.note: World leaders descend on the city for the high-level debate. Major security, traffic and diplomatic consequences for Midtown.

events.title: Housing in New York City, by Katrin B. Anacker
events.note: Traces the city from the 1811 grid through modern zoning, arguing New York set the national affordable-housing model and must reclaim it. (NYU Press)

events.title: What Architecture Is, by Witold Rybczynski
events.note: An essay, with 50 of the author's own drawings, on what separates architecture from mere building. (Yale)

events.title: Cold War City, by David E. Barclay
events.note: What five decades as a walled-in enclave did to West Berlin. (Princeton)

events.title: Stated meeting of the City Council
events.note: Council Chambers - City Hall
why: stated meeting of the full Council

events.title: Metropolitan Transportation Authority preliminary 2027 budget
events.note: A key indicator of fiscal health and service levels.

events.title: The Whitney's Breuer building opens on Madison Avenue — 60th anniversary
events.note: Marcel Breuer's granite-clad Whitney Museum opened at 945 Madison Avenue. Its inverted-ziggurat form and cantilevered upper floors made it a landmark of Brutalist architecture.

events.title: Man in the Mirror, by Anand Giridharadas
events.note: Reported narrative of the 2023 subway killing of Jordan Neely by Daniel Penny, and the failures on homelessness, mental illness and policing it exposed. (Knopf)

events.title: Federal fiscal year ends; shutdown risk
events.note: Federal appropriations expire at midnight. New York City is heavily exposed to disruptions in housing vouchers, Medicaid, transit and disaster aid.

events.title: Nunez monitor report to the federal court
events.note: The monitoring team told Judge Laura Taylor Swain it intends to file on this date, with an update on the state of the jails and on Local Law 42. The reports are the running scorecard on whether the city is curing its contempt.

events.title: New rent guidelines take effect
events.note: Order 58 takes effect for leases beginning Oct. 1, 2026 through Sept. 30, 2027. The board adopted a rent freeze — 0% on both one- and two-year leases — delivering the central promise of Mamdani's campaign.

events.title: Fair housing framework assessment due
events.note: The first Fair Housing Framework needs assessment and five-year affordable housing production targets for all 59 community districts, required by Charter section 16-a. Published every five years beginning October 2026.

events.title: The 12 lowest-producing community districts named
events.note: City Planning publishes the list of the 12 community districts producing the least affordable housing. It comes from the affordable housing fast track voters approved in November 2025 (Charter section 197-f), not from City of Yes. Projects there get an expedited process starting Jan. 1, 2027.

events.title: The People's Paper, by Matthew Pressman
events.note: A history of the Daily News from its 1919 launch to its collapse, arguing tabloids were essential to an informed working-class public. Publisher gives the month only. (Columbia)

events.title: Beyond Common Ground, by Daniel P. Aldrich
events.note: Argues parks and libraries outperform concrete for climate resilience, disaster recovery and crime reduction. Publisher gives the month only. (California)

events.title: Federal fiscal year 2027 begins
events.note: Watch for continuing resolutions and shutdown risk affecting city funding.

events.title: The Revolutionary, by Ross Barkan
events.note: A behind-the-scenes account of Mamdani's campaign and first months in office, and what his win did to the city's power establishment. (Random House)

events.title: Cities of Gods, by Jörg Rüpke
events.note: How urbanization and religion have made and remade each other, from Mesopotamia to the modern metropolis. (Princeton)

events.title: Stated meeting of the City Council
events.note: Council Chambers - City Hall
why: stated meeting of the full Council

events.title: Sanger opens first US birth control clinic — 110th anniversary
events.note: Margaret Sanger opened the country's first birth control clinic in the Brownsville section of Brooklyn.

events.title: Committee on Environmental Protection and Waterfronts
events.note: Committee Room - City Hall

events.title: Stated meeting of the City Council
events.note: Council Chambers - City Hall
why: stated meeting of the full Council

events.title: Voter registration deadline
events.note: Registration applications must be received by a board of elections by this date to vote in the Nov. 3 general election. Confirmed against the state Board of Elections' 2026 deadlines poster.

events.title: Early voting begins
events.note: The nine-day early voting period for the general election runs through Nov. 1. Polls are open 6 a.m. to 9 p.m. on Election Day itself.

events.title: Last day to request a mail ballot
events.note: The final day to apply for a general election mail ballot online or by mail. In person at a county board, the deadline is Nov. 2.

events.title: The Yankees' dynasty begins — 30th anniversary
events.note: The New York Yankees won the World Series over the Atlanta Braves, their first title since 1978 and the start of a late-1990s dynasty.

events.title: The Statue of Liberty is dedicated — 140th anniversary
events.note: President Grover Cleveland dedicated the Statue of Liberty on Bedloe's Island in New York Harbor.

events.title: The Battle of White Plains — 250th anniversary
events.note: British and Hessian forces under Howe defeated Washington at White Plains in Westchester on October 28, 1776.

events.title: New York City Marathon: 50 years of the five-borough course
events.note: The 55th running of the marathon, and the 50th anniversary of the five-borough course, which was first run in 1976 for the bicentennial. It is not the 50th five-borough running — that count is 49, because the race was cancelled in 2012 for Hurricane Sandy and in 2020 for COVID-19.

events.title: Early voting ends
events.note: The last of the nine early voting days before Tuesday's election.

events.title: Holding On to Home, by Sarah Bruhn
events.note: Five years of fieldwork on immigrant women resisting both immigration enforcement and displacement in a gentrifying city. Publisher gives the month only. (California)

events.title: General election
events.note: Governor: Hochul vs. likely Blakeman, Congress and state legislative races. No mayoral race this year. Hochul-Mamdani coordination is critical for Democratic turnout.

events.title: Mail ballots must be postmarked or returned
events.note: Completed mail ballots must be postmarked, or returned in person, by Election Day.

events.title: The Schaghen letter records the 'purchase' of Manhattan — 400th anniversary
events.note: A letter by Dutch official Pieter Schaghen, dated Nov. 5, 1626, reported that colonists had 'purchased' the island of Manhattan from its Native inhabitants for goods worth 60 guilders. It is the only contemporary document of the transaction.

events.title: District Council 37's contract expires
events.note: The 2021-2026 agreement for District Council 37, known as DC 37, covers nearly 90,000 city employees, about a quarter of the municipal workforce. DC 37 is the city's largest union, counting some 150,000 members across sectors. The deal that replaces this one sets the pattern for the teachers and everyone else, and is the first major labor test of this administration.

events.title: Bloomberg elected mayor after 9/11 — 25th anniversary
events.note: Media entrepreneur Michael Bloomberg won the mayoralty just weeks after the September 11 attacks, defeating Democrat Mark Green. He took office as the 108th mayor on Jan. 1, 2002.

events.title: Last day for mail ballots to arrive
events.note: Postmarked mail ballots must reach the county board of elections by this date to be counted. In close races, the result is not final until they are.

events.title: Renaissance Men, by Harriet A. Washington
events.note: Three Black New York physicians who reshaped medicine and culture between 1847 and 1952, and were written out of the record. (Little, Brown)

events.title: Stated meeting of the City Council
events.note: Council Chambers - City Hall
why: stated meeting of the full Council

events.title: Citywide statement of needs
events.note: Required by Charter section 204 each year by Nov. 15. It signals which city facilities the administration intends to open, close or expand — an early tell on siting fights to come.

events.title: The fall of Fort Washington — 250th anniversary
events.note: British and Hessian forces captured Fort Washington, the last American stronghold on Manhattan, on November 16, 1776.

events.title: Stated meeting of the City Council
events.note: Council Chambers - City Hall
why: stated meeting of the full Council

events.title: November financial plan update
events.note: The city updates revenue and spending projections, setting the baseline for the next budget cycle.

events.title: The 100th Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade
events.note: A milestone parade; the count reaches 100 in 2026 because the parade was suspended during World War II. A year-long celebration campaign surrounds it.

events.title: New York, 1860, by Joshua K. Leon
events.note: A month-by-month portrait of the city from John Brown's raid to Fort Sumter. Publisher gives the month only. (Columbia)

events.title: Notable New Yorkers of Harlem, by Jim Mackin
events.note: A biographical reference on more than 800 Harlem residents from the Dutch era to now, with addresses for walking tours. (Fordham)

events.title: Stated meeting of the City Council
events.note: Council Chambers - City Hall
why: stated meeting of the full Council

events.title: Build or Die, by Ned Resnikoff
events.note: Argues big American cities are failing because they cannot absorb growth, and makes the pro-building, pro-transit case. (Island Press)

events.title: UN chooses New York City for its headquarters — 80th anniversary
events.note: The United Nations General Assembly voted to site its permanent headquarters in Manhattan after John D. Rockefeller Jr. donated $8.5 million to buy the East River tract.

events.title: Nunez monitor report on the 22nd monitoring period
events.note: The monitor's December report, assessing compliance for the 22nd monitoring period. It effectively grades the administration's first year of jail management. Date approximate: the monitor has said December but has not fixed a day.

events.title: Metropolitan Transportation Authority board adopts the 2027 budget
events.note: The final operating budget for the coming year.

events.title: The Accidental Gentrifier, by Terry Shook
events.note: A veteran architect-planner on 50 years of revitalization projects that displaced the people they were meant to help. Date from a trade listing; the publisher gives December only. (BenBella)

events.title: Stated meeting of the City Council
events.note: Council Chambers - City Hall
why: stated meeting of the full Council

events.title: Albany post-election positioning
events.note: The lame-duck period for termed-out legislators; watch for late activity on pending bills.

events.title: Mamdani's first year in office
events.note: One year since the inaugural address of Jan. 1, 2026. A customary peg for retrospectives rather than a scheduled event.

events.title: Affordable housing fast track begins
events.note: Affordable housing projects in the 12 lowest-producing community districts get a shortened approval process, under the charter amendment voters approved in November 2025.

events.title: Rikers land transfer deadline
events.note: The semi-annual deadline to move unused Rikers land to the Department of Citywide Administrative Services, seven months before the island must close.

events.title: Policed, by Marisol LeBrón
events.note: How policing shaped Latinx identity and politics, tying together immigration, work, health and housing. Publisher gives the month only. (California)

events.title: Jury selection in Mangione's federal trial
events.note: Jury selection begins in the federal capital case before Judge Margaret Garnett. Opening statements are set for Jan. 25.

events.title: The state legislative session convenes
events.note: The Legislature returns to Albany. The session opens on the first Wednesday after the first Monday in January; the exact date should be confirmed against the Senate calendar. Session days will begin appearing on this calendar automatically once Albany publishes them.

events.title: No More Dual City, by Christina Jackson
events.note: An ethnography of Atlantic City's historically Black Northside on how the casino economy drives redevelopment and exclusion. (Rutgers)

events.title: Preliminary budget for fiscal 2028
events.note: Mamdani's second budget, due under Charter section 236. Note the February deadline does not apply: the extension exists only in the year following a mayoral election, which was 2026.

events.title: Draft ten-year capital strategy
events.note: Published alongside the preliminary budget in odd-numbered years under Charter section 228. It sets the decade's capital priorities — housing, transit, infrastructure — and is rarely watched relative to its consequences.

events.title: Making a Killing, by Robin D. G. Kelley
events.note: A history of police violence and Black resistance, from lynch law through segregated housing to gentrification. (Metropolitan)

events.title: Preliminary Mayor's Management Report
events.note: The mid-year performance report, required by Charter section 12 no later than Jan. 30.

events.title: State executive budget due
events.note: In the year following a gubernatorial election, the state constitution requires the governor's budget by Feb. 1. It determines city tax authority, transit funding and Medicaid cost-sharing — the single most consequential document of the Albany year.

events.title: Epic Harlem, by Herb Boyd
events.note: A sweeping narrative history of Harlem, from its earliest inhabitants to the gentrification era. (Fordham)

events.title: Council preliminary budget hearings conclude
events.note: The Council must hold hearings on the preliminary budget by this date under Charter section 247. Agency heads testify; it is the year's most sustained public interrogation of the administration.

events.title: State budget due
events.note: The state constitution requires a budget by April 1. It gates the money for child care and for fare-free buses, and it is the realistic vehicle for any millionaire or corporate tax increase, all of which require Albany.

events.title: Council's preliminary budget response
events.note: The Council's formal counter-document, due under Charter section 247. The opening position of the budget negotiation.

events.title: First Tony Awards honor Broadway — 80th anniversary
events.note: The American Theatre Wing held the first Antoinette Perry Awards in the Grand Ballroom of the Waldorf-Astoria, honoring excellence on Broadway. The awards were nicknamed the "Tonys" during the ceremony itself.

events.title: Roofless, by Karen A. Snedker and Jennifer McKinney
events.note: A study of a self-governed tent camp, and how unhoused residents build partnerships and self-governance. Rutgers has not published a date; the April date comes from a catalog listing and is unconfirmed. (Rutgers)

events.title: Jackie Robinson breaks baseball's color barrier — 80th anniversary
events.note: Jackie Robinson debuted at first base for the Brooklyn Dodgers, becoming the first Black player in the modern major leagues.

events.title: Studio 54 opens — 50th anniversary
events.note: The Midtown discotheque opened and instantly became the epicenter of celebrity nightlife and disco excess.

events.title: Ten-year capital strategy issued
events.note: The mayor's final capital strategy, due under Charter section 248 in odd-numbered years.

events.title: Lever House brings the glass curtain wall to Park Avenue — 75th anniversary
events.note: Gordon Bunshaft's Lever House, for Skidmore, Owings & Merrill, opened at 390 Park Avenue. Its blue-green glass skin was among the first true curtain walls on an American office tower.

events.title: Executive budget for fiscal 2028
events.note: The mayor's revised budget, due under Charter section 249, updated for whatever Albany did on April 1.

events.title: Local Law 97 annual emissions report due
events.note: Large buildings must file annual emissions reports each May 1. The first were due May 1, 2025, not 2026; penalties run about $268 per metric ton of carbon dioxide equivalent over a building's limit.

events.title: Rent Guidelines Board preliminary vote
events.note: The board votes on a range for the 2027-28 rent adjustment. Date estimated from recent practice: April 30 in 2024 and 2025, May 7 in 2026.

events.title: Trinity Church receives its royal charter — 330th anniversary
events.note: The Anglican parish of Trinity Church was chartered on May 6, 1697, on behalf of King William III by Royal Governor Benjamin Fletcher.

events.title: Live TV's Golden Age begins with Kraft Theatre — 80th anniversary
events.note: The premiere of Kraft Television Theatre on May 7, 1947 opened the Golden Age of live television drama, an era of anthology plays broadcast largely from New York studios.

events.title: Lindbergh gets a ticker-tape hero's welcome — 100th anniversary
events.note: Millions lined Broadway for a ticker-tape parade honoring Charles Lindbergh after his solo flight across the Atlantic.

events.title: Rent Guidelines Board final vote
events.note: The rent adjustment for roughly a million rent-stabilized apartments, for leases beginning October 2027. Date estimated from recent practice (June 17, 2024; June 30, 2025; June 25, 2026).

events.title: Fiscal 2028 budget adoption
events.note: The Charter's trigger date is June 5 (section 254), but in practice adoption lands at the end of June: the fiscal 2027 deal was struck and voted on June 30, 2026, hours before the deadline. Date estimated from that practice.

events.title: Slavery ends in New York State — 200th anniversary
events.note: An 1817 law took full effect on July 4, 1827, ending legal slavery in New York and freeing thousands of remaining enslaved people.

events.title: The blackout that broke the city's spirit — 50th anniversary
events.note: Lightning strikes knocked out power for some 25 hours, unleashing widespread looting and arson across the crime-plagued, cash-strapped city.

events.title: Silent Parade marches down Fifth Avenue — 110th anniversary
events.note: Thousands of Black New Yorkers marched in silence down Fifth Avenue to protest lynchings and the East St. Louis massacre.

events.title: The Son of Sam is captured — 50th anniversary
events.note: Police arrested David Berkowitz, ending a serial-killing spree that terrorized the city for more than a year.
